paper_title: Continual Unsupervised Domain Adaptation for Semantic Segmentation

methodology.problem: Pixel-level labeling for real-world images requires high time and labor costs
methodology.existing_approach: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (UDA) techniques adapt networks trained on synthetic images to real images
methodology.limitation: Most existing UDA methods focus on single-target settings, which is impractical for real-world scenarios
methodology.proposed_solution: Continual UDA scenario where a network trained on a source domain adapts to multiple target domains presented sequentially
methodology.key_challenge: Catastrophic forgetting when applying existing UDA methods to Continual UDA setting

components.name: Target-specific Memory (TM)
components.description: Lightweight sub-memory for each target domain containing unique information corresponding to each domain discrepancy

results.finding: Traditional UDA methods suffer from catastrophic forgetting when applied to Continual UDA tasks
results.improvement: ETM framework alleviates forgetting by expanding Target-specific Memory
results.validation: Qualitative results show that the framework mitigates forgetting on previously learned target domains

title: Continual Unsupervised Domain Adaptation for Semantic Segmentation
